title: Dark-Mode Restaurant Website Hero Food Photography Pack
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real Upwork brief (db_i 1214): a Portugal-based boutique digital agency building a "premium restaurant website" that is "high-end, clean, and modern minimalist" with a "Dark Mode aesthetic preferred, focusing heavily on high-quality food photography," with Home / Interactive Food Menu / Booking sections, mobile-responsive. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: the original post asks for an end-to-end Wix/Framer UI/UX build, which is NOT an Adobe-connector capability (no layout composition, no web build). I scope the task to the brief's explicit and repeatedly-stressed pillar — the dark-mode food photography that the whole site "focuses heavily on." The freelancer is hired as the food-photo retoucher who turns the restaurant's raw phone-and-DSLR plate shots into a cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack (hero, two menu tiles, one full-bleed mobile crop) plus a Firefly moodboard the agency's web designer drops into the Wix/Framer comp. No new claim contradicts the brief; the dark-mode look, food-photo emphasis, Home/Menu sections, and mobile optimization are all from the source text. The specific raw-file count, dish choices, and crop dimensions are reasonable enrichment needed to make the chain concrete and executable.
one_line_ask: Retouch a restaurant's raw plated-dish photos into a cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack (hero + menu tiles + mobile crop) and assemble a Firefly moodboard for the agency's web designer.
full_brief:
We are a Portugal-based boutique digital agency building a premium restaurant website. The design is high-end, clean, modern-minimalist, Dark Mode aesthetic, and focuses heavily on high-quality food photography across the Home and Interactive Food Menu sections, fully optimized for mobile. We do NOT need the site built here — our Wix Studio designer handles layout. We need a food-photo retoucher to turn the restaurant's raw plate shots into a polished, cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack.

Supplied raw assets: (1) a hero shot of a signature seared-scallop dish on a dark slate plate, shot slightly tilted under warm mixed restaurant lighting; (2) a flat-lay of a chocolate dessert on a marble board; (3) a close-up of a cocktail with garnish; plus the restaurant's existing flat wordmark logo as a PNG.

Deliverables:
1. A polished, perfectly level dark-mode HERO image — straightened, auto-toned, exposure/contrast set for a moody dark background, the plate subject made to "pop" off the dark surround, cooler-warmer balance dialed to a premium look, then reframed to the 16:9 hero size by cropping the plate off-center to the right so the dish's own near-black charcoal surround gives the web designer clean dark negative space on the left for an overlaid headline (edges cropped to hit the ratio). Export-ready full-bleed desktop hero.
2. Two square MENU-TILE images (dessert + cocktail) graded to match the hero's dark, moody, consistent look so the Interactive Food Menu feels cohesive.
3. One full-bleed 9:16 MOBILE crop of the hero, subject-aware, for the mobile Home section.
4. The flat wordmark logo cleaned to a transparent PNG and vectorized to crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site.
5. A Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the final hero, menu tiles, and logo as a single deep-link the Wix designer can reference for the dark-mode visual direction.

The whole set must read as one premium, dark, cohesive system. Match grade across all food images. Keep skin/food tones appetizing — dark mode, not muddy.

inputs.name: raw_hero_scallops.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal restaurant food photograph, raw and unretouched look, three pan-seared scallops with golden crust on a dark charcoal slate plate, micro-herb garnish and a smear of pea puree, shot on a dark wooden restaurant table under warm mixed tungsten lighting, slightly tilted horizon by about 4 degrees, slightly underexposed and flat contrast as if straight out of a DSLR, visible plate texture and real food detail, condensation and tiny oil sheen on the scallops, candid documentary food shot, no text, 3:2 landscape
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an untouched DSLR capture: deliberately tilted ~4deg so auto_straighten has work to do, slightly flat/underexposed so the tonal chain is justified, warm color cast for the temperature step. Real food micro-detail (sear marks, herb edges), no CGI gloss.

inputs.name: raw_dessert_flatlay.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal overhead flat-lay food photograph, raw unedited look, a molten chocolate fondant dessert dusted with cocoa on a grey marble board, a single raspberry and gold leaf garnish, soft daylight from a window so it reads slightly cooler and brighter than a moody restaurant shot, natural shadows, real crumb and melt detail, candid documentary style, no text, square 1:1
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately shot under different (cooler, brighter) light than the hero so the grade-matching step is meaningful. Square framing for a menu tile. Genuine dessert texture, no plastic sheen.

inputs.name: raw_cocktail_closeup.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal close-up food photograph, raw unedited look, an amber whiskey sour cocktail in a coupe glass with a citrus twist and a thin foam layer, condensation on the glass, dark bar background slightly out of focus, neutral-to-cool ambient light, real glass refraction and bubble detail, candid documentary style, no text, square 1:1
inputs.realism_notes: Neutral/cool ambient so it visibly differs from the warm hero before grade-matching. Square for the menu tile. Real condensation and refraction, not a render.

inputs.name: restaurant_wordmark_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A minimalist flat restaurant wordmark logo, the words rendered in an elegant thin serif typeface, solid near-black charcoal lettering on a plain flat white background, perfectly flat vector-style with crisp clean edges, small leaf glyph between two words, no gradients no shadows no photographic texture, centered, high resolution
inputs.realism_notes: Flat solid-color wordmark on white so remove_background (white drop-out) and then vectorize produce a clean SVG. Crisp edges and a single dark ink color make the raster-to-vector step honest and high-quality.

outputs.name: hero_dark_desktop.png
outputs.spec: Full-bleed desktop website hero, ~2880x1620 (16:9), straightened, dark-mode graded, subject popped off a moody dark surround, reframed by cropping the native 3:2 to 16:9 with the plate offset right so the existing dark surround forms clean headline negative space on the left (edges cropped to hit the ratio), sRGB PNG.

outputs.name: menu_tile_dessert.png
outputs.spec: Square 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.

outputs.name: menu_tile_cocktail.png
outputs.spec: Square 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.

outputs.name: hero_mobile_9x16.png
outputs.spec: Full-bleed mobile Home crop, 1080x1920 (9:16), subject-aware crop of the graded hero, sRGB PNG.

outputs.name: logo_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Transparent, cleanly vectorized wordmark logo as crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site (plus the transparent-PNG intermediate).

outputs.name: moodboard_deeplink
outputs.spec: Firefly Boards deep-link URL assembling the final hero, both menu tiles, and the logo as one dark-mode visual-direction reference for the Wix designer.

connector_workflow.note: Client supplies raw_hero_scallops.jpg. Initialize the upload session for the raw hero plate shot to bring it into CC storage so the image pipeline can act on it.
Finalize the upload from step 1's session to get a stored CC asset/URI for the raw hero. This is the loaded source for the hero chain.
Level the ~4deg tilted horizon on the finalized hero asset. Consumes step 2's output.
Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/underexposed raw capture. Consumes step 3.
Pull the background down and add contrast on the auto-toned hero to establish the dark-mode moody base look. Consumes step 4.
Dial the warm tungsten cast to the premium target balance on the dark-graded hero. This graded hero becomes the reference look that the menu tiles must match. Consumes step 5.
Select the scallop plate subject on the graded hero to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 6 and produces a mask URI.
Apply vibrance/saturation to the graded hero using step 7's subject mask so the plate pops off the dark surround (selective masked edit). Consumes the step-6 image + step-7 mask.

Reframe the popped 3:2 hero to a wider 16:9 by cropping with the scallop plate offset to the RIGHT so the existing dark charcoal surround on the LEFT becomes clean headline negative space. Honest crop only — no canvas is grown beyond the source and no pixels are invented; edges get cropped to hit the 16:9 ratio. Consumes step 8.
Resize the reframed 16:9 hero to the ~2880x1620 desktop full-bleed export dimensions. DELIVERABLE hero_dark_desktop.png. Consumes step 9.
Subject-aware crop of the finished desktop hero to a 9:16 1080x1920 mobile full-bleed for the mobile Home section. DELIVERABLE hero_mobile_9x16.png. Consumes step 10's output (reusing the hero).
Client supplies raw_dessert_flatlay.jpg. Bring the raw dessert flat-lay into CC storage to start the first menu tile.
Auto-tone the dessert raw to normalize its brighter/cooler daylight capture before matching. Consumes step 12.
Pull down and add contrast to match the hero's dark moody base (same grade as step 5) so the dessert tile reads cohesively. Consumes step 13.
Warm the cool dessert toward the hero's temperature target to finish grade-matching; output is the square menu tile. DELIVERABLE menu_tile_dessert.png. Consumes step 14.
Client supplies raw_cocktail_closeup.jpg. Bring the raw cocktail close-up into CC storage to start the second menu tile.
Apply a dark/moody Lightroom preset (from list_presets) to the cocktail raw to bring it toward the hero's grade in one consistent move. Consumes step 16.
Nudge the cocktail temperature to lock it to the hero/dessert balance so all three food images match; output is the square menu tile. DELIVERABLE menu_tile_cocktail.png. Consumes step 17.
Client supplies restaurant_wordmark_logo.png. Bring the flat wordmark logo into CC storage for cleanup and vectorization.
Drop the flat white background to a transparent PNG so the wordmark sits cleanly on the dark site. Consumes step 19.
Vectorize the transparent wordmark to crisp SVG for retina/responsive use. DELIVERABLE logo_wordmark.svg. Consumes step 20.

Assemble the final hero, both graded menu tiles, and the vectorized logo into a Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link as the dark-mode visual-direction reference for the Wix designer. DELIVERABLE moodboard_deeplink. Consumes the finished outputs of steps 10, 15, 18, 21.

chaining_note: Strict output to input chaining, three converging tributaries feeding one moodboard. HERO tributary: upload raw (1 to 2) to hero_asset_cc to auto_straighten (3) to apply_auto_tone (4) to brightness_contrast (5, sets the dark base) to color_temperature (6, the reference grade) to select_subject (7, mask off the graded hero) to vibrance_and_saturation (8, masked pop using step 6 image + step 7 mask) to crop_and_resize (9, reframe 3:2 to 16:9 with the subject offset right so the existing dark surround becomes left headline negative space) to crop_and_resize (10, resize to ~2880x1620 = hero_dark_desktop). The mobile crop (11) reuses step 10's finished hero. DESSERT tributary: add_file (12) to auto_tone (13) to brightness_contrast (14, same dark base as step 5) to color_temperature (15, matched to the hero target = menu_tile_dessert). COCKTAIL tributary: add_file (16) to apply_preset (17, dark/moody look) to color_temperature (18, locked to the same balance = menu_tile_cocktail). LOGO tributary: add_file (19) to remove_background (20, transparent PNG) to vectorize (21, logo_wordmark_svg). FINAL: create_firefly_board (22) consumes the four finished outputs (steps 10, 15, 18, 21) into the deliverable deep-link. Every step after each initial asset load consumes a named prior output; grade consistency is enforced because steps 14/15/17/18 deliberately mirror the hero's step 5/6 grade.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 22 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 2 capability groups (A1 raster, A4 asset/board). It is a genuine food_bev_photo retouch job — the hardest, most realistic part of the long-tail of the actual brief. Difficulty comes from (a) a 10-step single-image hero pipeline including a masked selective edit (select_subject to vibrance on the mask) and a two-stage gen-free reframe (crop 3:2 to 16:9 with the subject offset so the existing dark surround becomes left headline negative space, then resize to the export dims), (b) grade-MATCHING across three differently-lit raw captures so the menu reads cohesively (the core 'cohesive dark-mode system' requirement), and (c) a converging dependency graph where four sub-chains must all complete before the moodboard step. Every step is headless-confirmed [C]: no [W] Express widget, no [A] async video/audio, no [T] authored template, no Canva, no local step. No not-available capability is used — no generative-expand/outpaint, no image_fill_area misuse, no compositing/gen-fill/object-removal/upscale/text-to-image, and no layout composition (no render_layout/render_vector as authoring). The web build itself is correctly out of scope and excluded.

reverify.banned_capability_check: NO banned capability remains. The former banned step (n=9 image_crop_and_resize) had been left mis-tasked to image_fill_area (which cannot enlarge a 3:2 frame to a wider 16:9 or add new left-side space); it is now replaced with image_crop_and_resize, a gen-free reframe that CROPS the 3:2 popped hero to 16:9 with the subject offset right so the existing dark charcoal surround forms left headline negative space — the canvas is never grown beyond the source. No generative-expand/outpaint, gen-fill, upscale/super-resolution, text-to-image, from-scratch art/logo, object-removal-with-reconstruction, or render_* layout-authoring anywhere. remove_background (white drop-out to transparent PNG), image_vectorize (raster to SVG), and select_subject to masked vibrance edit are all standard A1 [C] capabilities. All 22 steps headless-confirmed [C], server=adobe.
reverify.notes: Only step 9 changed tool (image_fill_area -> image_crop_and_resize) plus its output name (hero_expanded -> hero_reframed_16x9); step 10 now consumes hero_reframed_16x9 and finalizes the ~2880x1620 export. tool_call_count stays 22 and distinct_adobe_tools drops to 15 (image_fill_area no longer used; image_crop_and_resize already counted). Downstream chaining intact: step 10 hero_dark_desktop still feeds mobile crop (11) and moodboard (22). Deliverable intent (moody dark-mode full-bleed 16:9 hero with clean left negative space) and target dims preserved. chaining_note and difficulty_rationale describe the crop-based reframe with no generative-expand/image_fill_area references; full_brief Deliverable 1 and the hero output spec were reworded from 'placed on a solid charcoal canvas' to an honest off-center crop of the native 3:2 to 16:9 (edges cropped to hit the ratio) so no canvas-extension is implied; JSON validated.
